pattern: Membership in the target set only depends on divisibility by three small numbers, so a single sweep from one to n that adds any qualifying value builds the total directly.
hints: A number qualifies the moment it is divisible by any one of three fixed small divisors.
You only need to look at each integer from one up to n exactly once.
Keep a running total and add the current integer whenever it qualifies.

edge_inputs.description: small n covering all three divisors
n is exactly the least common multiple of all three divisors
n is a multiple of exactly two of the divisors at once
n is a multiple of the largest divisor only
n just below the first qualifying value

stress.description: random n across the full range
